**SGSC-AI Intelligence Probe Executed**

Triggering the system-level 'dictionary-style license' stripping and '22-employee non-standard automation equipment assembly plant' physical confirmation probe. The fake Apple multinational foundry empire template in the original data has been completely shattered. This enterprise, named 'Siman Smart Equipment', has a business license nearly a thousand words long, which can be called an encyclopedia of China's mechanical and electronic industrial classification: from 'integrated circuit manufacturing' to 'industrial robot manufacturing', and then to 'metal cutting and welding equipment manufacturing' and 'smart material handling equipment sales', almost covering all aspects of human industry. The official system was unsurprisingly intimidated by it, classifying it into 'Small Enterprises' and 'Electronic Device Manufacturing'. However, the forensic logic engine directly intercepted its real physical foundation: the number of insured employees is 22! The paid-in capital of 5.2 million is fully in place. Most crucially, its spatial coordinates are located in 'Building B Factory, Taiping Industrial Park... Jiangning District'. The logical deduction rapidly converged: this is absolutely not a ten-billion-level giant capable of building 'chips' or 'metal cutting machine tools', but similarly, this is not a shell company either. With 22 workers and over 5 million in capital, inside a real industrial park factory, what they do is extremely typical 'Custom Automation Equipment Assembly & Integration'. The probe mercilessly deleted the extremely high-threshold words like 'integrated circuit manufacturing, forming machine tool manufacturing' used in the license just to pad the word count and hold a spot, extracting the work these 22 people can actually do in the factory: 'smart basic manufacturing equipment manufacturing; mechanical and electrical equipment manufacturing; electrical installation services; industrial automatic control system device manufacturing'. The truth is extremely grounded: this is a real '22-employee physical factory / non-standard automated testing / assembly equipment and control cabinet assembly plant'. According to the needs of customers (large factories), they purchase pneumatic components, servo motors, and control boards, and assemble them in the workshop into dedicated automated test benches or small assembly line equipment. This thousand-word 'dictionary-style license' is merely an extreme 'defensive positioning' by the boss to facilitate taking on automation retrofitting projects under various different names.
